components.name: GraphBlockAnchor
components.description: Renders an anchor (connection point) for a graph block, positioning it on the canvas and managing selection/raised state

props.name: graph
props.description: Graph instance

props.name: anchor
props.description: Anchor data object

props.name: position
props.description: CSS position mode for anchor placement

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class name

props.name: children
props.description: Render content or render function receiving anchor state

components.name: GraphBlock
components.description: Base block component for rendering HTML blocks via React. Creates a container for the graph block, correctly places it on the canvas, updates geometry and z-index when state changes. By default hides canvas block automatically when React renders the block. Supports forwardRef — attach a ref to access the outer container HTMLDivElement.

props.name: graph
props.description: Graph instance

props.name: block
props.description: Block instance

props.name: className
props.description: Class name for wrapper div. Applied to the inner wrapper div around block content.

props.name: containerClassName
props.description: Class name for container div. Applied to the outer container div for position and size.

props.name: autoHideCanvas
props.description: Flag to hide canvas block automatically when React renders the block. Set to false to manage canvas block visibility manually.

props.name: canvasVisible
props.description: Flag to manage visibility of canvas block. Use with autoHideCanvas=false for manual visibility control.

props.name: children
props.description: Block content to render inside the wrapper

props.name: ref
props.description: Ref forwarded to the outer container div via forwardRef

components.name: Block
components.description: Memo component used internally by BlocksList to render a single block via a render function and block state. Exported from BlocksList.tsx but not re-exported from the package barrel — use BlocksList or GraphCanvas instead.

props.name: blockState
props.description: Reactive block state object

props.name: graphObject
props.description: Graph instance

props.name: renderBlock
props.type: <T extends TBlock>(graphObject: Graph, block: T, blockState: BlockState<T>) => React.JSX.Element
props.description: Render function for the block content

components.name: BlocksList
components.description: Optimized list component that renders visible blocks at the detailed zoom level. Uses viewport culling and Set-based diffing for performance.

props.name: graphObject
props.description: Graph instance

props.name: renderBlock
props.description: Render function called for each visible block

components.name: GraphCanvas
components.description: Main graph container component. Attaches the graph to the DOM, sets up event handlers, manages colors, and renders the React layer portal.

props.name: graph
props.description: Graph instance

props.name: className
props.description: CSS class for the graph wrapper

props.name: blockListClassName
props.description: CSS class for the block list container

props.name: renderBlock
props.description: Render function for blocks

props.name: reactLayerRef
props.description: Ref to access the internal ReactLayer instance

props.name: children
props.description: Additional React elements to render inside the graph wrapper

props.name: click
props.type: (data: { target?: EventedComponent; sourceEvent: Event; pointerPressed?: boolean }, event: GraphMouseEvent) => void
props.description: Fired when the graph canvas is clicked. Receives event detail with optional target component and source DOM event.

props.name: dblclick
props.type: (data: { target?: EventedComponent; sourceEvent: Event; pointerPressed?: boolean }, event: GraphMouseEvent) => void
props.description: Fired when the graph canvas is double-clicked. Receives event detail with optional target component and source DOM event.

props.name: onCameraChange
props.description: Fired when the camera state changes (pan, zoom). Receives the new camera state.

props.name: onBlockDragStart
props.type: (data: { nativeEvent: MouseEvent; block: TBlock }, event: CustomEvent<{ nativeEvent: MouseEvent; block: TBlock }>) => void
props.description: Fired when a block drag operation starts. Receives the native mouse event and the block being dragged.

props.name: onBlockDrag
props.type: (data: { nativeEvent: MouseEvent; block: TBlock; x: number; y: number }, event: CustomEvent<{ nativeEvent: MouseEvent; block: TBlock; x: number; y: number }>) => void
props.description: Fired continuously while a block is being dragged. Receives the native mouse event, the block, and the new x/y coordinates.

props.name: onBlockDragEnd
props.type: (data: { nativeEvent: MouseEvent; block: TBlock }, event: CustomEvent<{ nativeEvent: MouseEvent; block: TBlock }>) => void
props.description: Fired when a block drag operation ends. Receives the native mouse event and the block that was dragged.

props.name: onBlockSelectionChange
props.description: Fired when the block selection changes. Receives a selection diff with added and removed block IDs.

props.name: onBlockAnchorSelectionChange
props.type: (data: { anchor: TAnchor; selected: boolean }, event: CustomEvent<{ anchor: TAnchor; selected: boolean }>) => void
props.description: Fired when a block anchor selection changes. Receives the anchor and its new selection state.

props.name: onBlockChange
props.type: (data: { block: TBlock }, event: CustomEvent<{ block: TBlock }>) => void
props.description: Fired when a block is changed (e.g. moved). Receives the updated block data.

props.name: onConnectionSelectionChange
props.description: Fired when the connection selection changes. Receives a selection diff with added and removed connection IDs.

props.name: onStateChanged
props.type: (data: { state: GraphState }, event: CustomEvent<{ state: GraphState }>) => void
props.description: Fired when the graph state changes. Receives the new graph state.

components.name: GraphContextProvider
components.description: React context provider that makes a Graph instance available to child components via useGraphContext

props.name: graph
props.description: Graph instance to provide via context

props.name: children
props.description: Child elements that can access the graph context

components.name: GraphLayer
components.description: Declarative component for adding existing Layer classes to the graph. Provides a React-friendly way to register and manage graph layers.

props.name: layer
props.description: Layer constructor class

props.name: props
props.description: Props to pass to layer constructor

props.name: ref
props.description: Ref to access layer instance

components.name: GraphPortal
components.description: Declarative component for creating HTML layers using render prop pattern. Creates an internal Layer with an HTML element and renders React components through React Portal without needing a separate Layer class. Supports forwardRef — attach a ref to access the internal GraphPortalLayer instance.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS classes for layer

props.name: zIndex
props.description: Layer position on Z axis

props.name: transformByCameraPosition
props.description: Whether HTML element should follow camera position

props.name: children
props.description: Static content or render function receiving the layer and graph instances

props.name: ref
props.description: Ref forwarded to the internal GraphPortalLayer instance via forwardRef

hooks.name: useGraph
hooks.signature: useGraph(config: HookGraphParams): { graph: Graph; api: PublicGraphApi; setSettings: Function; start: Function; stop: Function; setViewConfiguration: Function; addLayer: Function; setEntities: Function; updateEntities: Function; zoomTo: Function }
hooks.description: Creates and manages a Graph instance with configuration, settings, and convenience methods for controlling the graph lifecycle

parameters.name: config
parameters.description: Graph configuration including settings, layers, viewConfiguration, and optional existing graph instance

hooks.return_type: { graph: Graph; api: PublicGraphApi; setSettings: Function; start: Function; stop: Function; setViewConfiguration: Function; addLayer: Function; setEntities: Function; updateEntities: Function; zoomTo: Function }

hooks.name: useLayer
hooks.signature: useLayer<T extends Constructor<Layer>>(graph: Graph | null, layerCtor: T, props: LayerPublicProps<T>): InstanceType<T> | null
hooks.description: Manages graph layers with automatic initialization, cleanup on unmount, and deep props comparison to optimize re-renders

parameters.name: props
parameters.description: Layer properties (excluding internal props like root, camera, graph, emitter)

hooks.name: useSignal
hooks.description: Subscribes to a Preact signal and returns its current value, re-rendering the component when the signal changes

hooks.name: useComputedSignal
hooks.description: Subscribes to a computed signal derived from a compute function and dependency list

hooks.name: useSignalEffect
hooks.description: Runs a side effect that tracks signal dependencies, re-executing when tracked signals or deps change

parameters.name: deps
parameters.description: Dependencies that recreate the effect when they change

hooks.name: useGraphEvent
hooks.signature: useGraphEvent<Event extends keyof GraphEventsDefinitions>(graph: Graph | null, event: Event, cb: (data: UnwrapGraphEventsDetail<Event>, event: UnwrapGraphEvents<Event>) => void, debounceParams?: { priority?: ESchedulerPriority; frameInterval?: number; frameTimeout?: number }): void
hooks.description: Subscribes to a graph event with automatic cleanup and optional debouncing via the scheduler

parameters.name: cb
parameters.description: Callback receiving event detail and the full event

parameters.name: debounceParams
parameters.type: { priority?: ESchedulerPriority; frameInterval?: number; frameTimeout?: number }
parameters.description: Optional debounce configuration for the event handler

hooks.name: useGraphEvents
hooks.signature: useGraphEvents(graph: Graph | null, events: Partial<TGraphEventCallbacks>): void
hooks.description: Subscribes to multiple graph events at once using a callback map with automatic cleanup

parameters.name: events
parameters.description: Object mapping event callback names to handler functions

hooks.name: useBlockState
hooks.signature: useBlockState<T extends TBlock>(graph: Graph, block: T | T["id"]): BlockState<T> | undefined
hooks.description: Returns the reactive BlockState for a given block or block ID, re-rendering when the block state changes

hooks.name: useSyncBlockState
hooks.signature: useSyncBlockState<T extends TBlock>(graph: Graph, block: T | T["id"]): BlockState<T> | undefined
hooks.description: Returns the current BlockState synchronously without subscribing to signal updates

hooks.name: useBlockViewState
hooks.signature: useBlockViewState<T extends TBlock>(graph: Graph, block: T | T["id"]): Block | undefined
hooks.description: Returns the canvas Block view component for a block (the low-level Canvas rendering component), or undefined if the block has no view component attached yet

hooks.name: useBlockAnchorState
hooks.signature: useBlockAnchorState(graph: Graph, anchor: TAnchor): AnchorState | undefined
hooks.description: Returns the reactive AnchorState for a given anchor, looking it up via the parent block state

hooks.name: useBlockAnchorPosition
hooks.signature: useBlockAnchorPosition(state: AnchorState | undefined, anchorContainerRef: React.MutableRefObject<HTMLDivElement> | undefined): void
hooks.description: Syncs an anchor's position CSS variables to its container element based on the anchor view component position

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Anchor state to track position from

parameters.name: anchorContainerRef
parameters.description: Ref to the anchor container DOM element

hooks.name: useSceneChange
hooks.description: Subscribes to scene updates triggered by camera changes or component position changes, with scheduler-based debouncing

hooks.name: useSchedulerDebounce
hooks.signature: useSchedulerDebounce<T extends (...args: Parameters<T>) => void>(fn: T, options: TDebounceOptions): T & { cancel: () => void }
hooks.description: Creates a debounced function that delays execution until both frame count and time conditions are met

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Configuration with priority, frameInterval, and frameTimeout

hooks.name: useSchedulerThrottle
hooks.signature: useSchedulerThrottle<T extends (...args: Parameters<T>) => void>(fn: T, options: TDebounceOptions): T & { cancel: () => void }
hooks.description: Creates a throttled function that limits execution frequency based on frame count and time conditions

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Configuration with priority, frameInterval, and frameTimeout

hooks.name: useScheduledTask
hooks.signature: useScheduledTask<T extends TSchedulerTaskFn>(fn: T, options: Omit<TScheduleOptions, "once">): void
hooks.description: Schedules a task for execution after a specified number of frames, with automatic cleanup on unmount

parameters.name: fn
parameters.description: The function to schedule for execution

hooks.name: useGraphContext
hooks.description: Returns the Graph instance from the nearest GraphCanvas context. Throws if used outside of GraphCanvas.

hooks.name: useElk
hooks.signature: useElk(config: ElkNode, elk: InstanceType<typeof ELK>, args?: ElkLayoutArguments & { onError?: (e: Error) => void }): { result: ConverterResult | null; isLoading: boolean }
hooks.description: Computes ELK graph layout asynchronously and returns the converted result with loading state

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Optional layout arguments and error handler
